deep_dive.title: When Is a Draft Accepted? A Theory of Acceptance in Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates language model inference by using a fast drafter to propose candidate tokens that are then verified by a larger target model. Existing theory largely studies the stochastic, distribution-preserving setting, where the goal is to exactly sample from the target distribution. In contrast, many practical systems use greedy decoding, relaxed acceptance rules, or tree-based candidate sets, where success is governed by local ranking and threshold events rather than exact distributional equality. We develop a theory for these regimes. We identify that many common acceptance criteria have rejection regions that can be characterized as lower level sets of the target distribution. For these, we characterize the exact KL divergence required for rejection yielding exact certificates and sharp margin-based bounds for strict greedy decoding, additive and multiplicative relaxed acceptance, top-(m) relaxed criteria, and entropy-thresholded acceptance. We then extend the framework to greedy tree decoding, deriving exact and margin-only certificates for when the target greedy token remains covered by the drafter's top-(m) candidates. Finally, we evaluate the resulting certificates on Qwen3 models, showing that relaxed and tree-based criteria substantially enlarge the region of certified acceptance, especially on decoding steps with low target model distribution margin. These results complement existing distribution-preserving analyses of speculative decoding by characterizing the deterministic local acceptance events common in practical inference systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: develops theory for acceptance criteria in speculative decoding, including relaxed rules
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 发展了投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 发展了投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 发展了投机解码中接受准则的理论，包括宽松规则
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops a theory for acceptance in speculative decoding beyond exact distribution matching, covering greedy decoding, relaxed rules, and tree-based candidates, characterizing rejection regions as lower level sets and analyzing KL divergence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码通常研究精确采样场景，但实际系统常采用贪心解码、宽松接受规则或树状候选集。论文对此类场景建立理论，识别出常见接受标准的拒绝域可表示为目标分布的下水平集，并刻画了其中的KL散度。该工作为理解投机解码的验证策略提供了理论基础。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Predict, Reuse, and Repair: Accelerating Dynamic Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Dynamic sparse attention (DSA) accelerates long-context LLM decoding by attending to only the top-K KV blocks relevant to each query, but it introduces a serialized selection-to-attention dependency that emerges as a new latency bottleneck. We present PRR, a speculate-reuse-repair runtime that exploits temporal locality in DSA selections to predict likely blocks, speculate the attention over them while selection is in flight, and incrementally repair missed blocks once the true selected set is known. PRR uses a lightweight EMA-based predictor, a profiling-guided speculation budget that keeps speculative work off the critical path, and a FlashAttention-based repair kernel that folds missed blocks into the partial attention state using online-softmax statistics. Across long-context benchmarks and representative DSA methods, PRR reduces per-token decoding latency by up to 40% while preserving downstream task accuracy. Github: https://github.com/Tianyu9748/Incremental_FlashAttention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculate-reuse-repair runtime accelerates dynamic sparse attention for LLM decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 投机-重用-修复运行时加速LLM解码的动态稀疏注意力
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PRR accelerates long-context LLM decoding by speculatively attending to predicted KV blocks, reusing partial results, and repairing misses, reducing latency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 动态稀疏注意力在长上下文LLM解码中引入串行选择-依赖瓶颈。本文提出PRR运行时，利用时间局部性预测KV块，在选择过程中投机执行注意力计算，随后增量修复遗漏的块。PRR包含轻量EMA预测器、分析引导的投机预算和基于FlashAttention的修复核。在多个长上下文基准上显著降低延迟，展示了投机执行在LLM推理加速中的潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 动态稀疏注意力在长上下文LLM解码中引入串行选择-依赖瓶颈。本文提出PRR运行时，利用时间局部性预测KV块，在选择过程中投机执行注意力计算，随后增量修复遗漏的块。PRR包含轻量EMA预测器、分析引导的投机预算和基于FlashAttention的修复核。在多个长上下文基准上显著降低延迟，展示了投机执行在LLM推理加速中的潜力。

deep_dive.title: SeKV: Resolution-Adaptive KV Cache with Hierarchical Semantic Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models increasingly operate over long contexts, where the KV cache becomes a dominant memory bottleneck: its size grows linearly with sequence length and must be retained throughout decoding, making full GPU caching prohibitively expensive without compression. Existing KV cache compression methods struggle to balance efficiency with faithful context preservation. Token eviction discards information, while semantic grouping fixes compression decisions at prefill time; neither can recover token-level detail from a compressed span once it becomes relevant during generation. As a solution, we propose SeKV, a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that organizes context into entropy-guided semantic spans and stores them across a GPU-CPU memory hierarchy without discarding information. Each span keeps a lightweight summary vector on GPU for coarse routing and a low-rank SVD basis on CPU for on-demand token-level reconstruction. A trained zoom-in mechanism selectively expands query-relevant spans during decoding, enabling precise retrieval without materializing the full KV cache on GPU. SeKV enables adaptive token-level reconstruction while keeping the base LLM fully frozen and adding fewer than 0.05% trainable parameters. Across four benchmarks, SeKV improves over the strongest semantic compression baseline by 5.9% on average while reducing GPU memory by 53.3% versus full KV caching at 128K context. Code is available on https://github.com/AmirAbaskohi/SeKV.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache optimization for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: KV缓存优化用于高效LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: KV缓存优化用于高效LLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: KV缓存优化用于高效LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SeKV proposes a resolution-adaptive semantic KV cache that compresses long-context LLM inference with hierarchical semantic memory and entropy-guided span storage, overcoming token-level recovery limits of prior methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对长上下文LLM推理中KV缓存内存瓶颈严重且现有压缩方法无法恢复细节的问题，SeKV提出了一种分辨率自适应的语义KV缓存，通过熵引导的语义跨度组织和层次化语义记忆，在压缩的同时保留恢复能力，从而在内存效率和上下文保真度之间取得更好平衡。

quick_skim.title: Speculative Pre-Positioning: Decoding Stateful Sessions to the Next Decision Point Off the Critical Path
quick_skim.abstract: A stateless inference server (vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM) idles between requests while the accelerator waits; a stateful session reclaims that idle time. Speculative pre-positioning decodes the session forward to its next decision point with the target model's own forward pass and no draft model, moving the cross-request prefill and entry-decode off the critical path: the next request resumes from a pre-paid entry on its delta, or, when a confidence gate fires, is answered from a cached distribution in one near-constant vocabulary scan with no decode, at a cost only of energy and a rare, bounded false accept. The payoff is conditional on capability: a capable model fires the gate at near-full coverage and about 87% precision (a smaller one never clears it), returning the first token in about 1.0 ms versus the 39 ms decode a prefix cache still pays.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speculative pre-positioning with confidence gate for relaxed verification in LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于置信度门的投机预定位，用于LLM推理中的宽松验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于置信度门的投机预定位，用于LLM推理中的宽松验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于置信度门的投机预定位，用于LLM推理中的宽松验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Speculative pre-positioning decodes stateful sessions off the critical path using the target model alone, with a confidence gate for relaxed acceptance, reducing latency for LLM serving.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 无状态推理服务器在请求间空闲，而有状态会话可被利用。本文提出投机预定位，使用目标模型自身的前向传播在空闲时预解码会话到下一个决策点，通过置信度门实现宽松接受（当置信度高时直接从缓存分布中回答，无需解码），将跨请求预填充和入口解码移出关键路径，从而降低延迟。实验表明在高容量模型下可达近全覆盖和约87%的预。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 无状态推理服务器在请求间空闲，而有状态会话可被利用。本文提出投机预定位，使用目标模型自身的前向传播在空闲时预解码会话到下一个决策点，通过置信度门实现宽松接受（当置信度高时直接从缓存分布中回答，无需解码），将跨请求预填充和入口解码移出关键路径，从而降低延迟。实验表明在高容量模型下可达近全覆盖和约87%的预。

quick_skim.title: Depth Exploration for LLM Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive LLM decoding evaluates every generated token through the full layer stack, even though many tokens become predictable at intermediate depths. Existing lossless depth-adaptive methods exploit this redundancy by choosing a single non-final exit depth and verifying its prediction with the final-depth model. However, our measurements show that this selection-based strategy leaves substantial headroom: choosing an exit too late wastes computation, while choosing one too early triggers fallback and discards dependent drafts. We propose Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX), a lossless decoding algorithm that replaces single-depth selection with parallel exploration over multiple candidate depths. At each commit position, DEX validates candidates against the final-depth reference, commits exactly the final-depth token, and collapses the exploration lattice to retain only reusable branch states. This expand--commit--collapse procedure preserves equivalence to standard autoregressive decoding while reducing the cost of committing each token. Across early-exit-trained and standard LLMs, DEX outperforms representative depth-selection baselines and achieves competitive end-to-end throughput against speculative and distributed decoding methods. Moreover, DEX improves as the explored depths become finer, showing that parallel depth exploration provides a scalable way to exploit the underused depth axis of LLM decoding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: explores parallel depth exploration and verification for LLM decoding, reducing computation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 探索LLM解码中的并行深度探索与验证，减少计算量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 探索LLM解码中的并行深度探索与验证，减少计算量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 探索LLM解码中的并行深度探索与验证，减少计算量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Depth Exploration Decoding (DEX) reduces LLM decoding cost by validating multiple intermediate depths in parallel against the final layer, achieving lossless acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的LLM解码每步都要计算全部层，深度自适应方法只选一个中间层退出但可能浪费计算或触发回退。本文提出深度探索解码(DEX)，并行探索多个候选深度并同时验证它们与最终层的一致性，避免单深度选择的风险。实验表明DEX在保持无损失的前提下显著加速解码，为LLM推理效率提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: LASER: A Corrective Lens for LVLMs via Visual Attention Preservation and Sink Suppression
quick_skim.abstract: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) exhibit strong reasoning ability but suffer from visual forgetting during long-horizon decoding, where attention progressively drifts away from visual evidence. Existing methods largely treat this issue as a late-stage attention decay problem or attempt to mitigate it through heuristic reminders or post-hoc attention lifting. Through systematic empirical analysis, we find that performance degradation under visual forgetting is largely driven by two overlooked factors: early-stage attention decay disrupts evidence acquisition, and attention concentration on a subset of task-irrelevant visual sink tokens. Motivated by these insights, we propose LASER, a post-training framework that regulates both the visual attention trajectory and intra-visual token attention distribution during reasoning. Technically, LASER introduces two complementary rewards: a Visual Grounding Reward, which encourages the model to maintain attention on semantically salient visual tokens throughout decoding, and a Sink Suppression Reward, which penalizes excessive attention concentration on visual sink tokens. Together, these rewards preserve early-stage grounding while preventing attention collapse onto uninformative regions. Extensive experiments on eight benchmark datasets demonstrate that LASER consistently outperforms strong baselines, validating attention-aware training as an effective remedy for visual forgetting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improves LVLM inference by preserving visual attention
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过保持视觉注意力改善LVLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过保持视觉注意力改善LVLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过保持视觉注意力改善LVLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LASER addresses visual forgetting in LVLMs by preserving early attention and suppressing sink tokens, improving inference quality without extra costs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大型视觉语言模型在长序列解码中会出现视觉遗忘，导致性能下降。LASER通过分析发现早期注意力衰减和视觉sink token集中是主要原因，并提出一个后训练框架，同时调节早期注意力保持和sink token抑制，从而在不增加推理成本的情况下提升模型在长视觉上下文中的表现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大型视觉语言模型在长序列解码中会出现视觉遗忘，导致性能下降。LASER通过分析发现早期注意力衰减和视觉sink token集中是主要原因，并提出一个后训练框架，同时调节早期注意力保持和sink token抑制，从而在不增加推理成本的情况下提升模型在长视觉上下文中的表现。

quick_skim.title: An Efficient vLLM-Based Inference Pipeline for Unified Audio Understanding and Generation
quick_skim.abstract: While Large Multimodal Models excel in comprehension, high-throughput inference engines lack native support for multimodal generation. This is severe in Speech Language Models, where generating multi-layered audio tokens via decoupled AR+NAR or synchronous Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) with delay-pattern interleaving conflicts with standard single-stream loops. We present a vLLM-based inference pipeline for unified speech understanding and generation. We extend autoregressive decoding to natively execute delay-pattern de-interleaving and coordinated multi-stream sampling, integrating an on-GPU acoustic decoder for end-to-end waveform synthesis. Crucially, we overcome the shared intuition that Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) halves throughput. By co-scheduling paired conditional and unconditional requests within a continuous batch, our CFG implementation sustains 80% of non-CFG throughput, absorbing dual-request and logit merging overheads. We open-source our framework.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient vLLM inference pipeline for multimodal generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于多模态生成的高效vLLM推理流水线
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于多模态生成的高效vLLM推理流水线
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于多模态生成的高效vLLM推理流水线
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a vLLM-based inference pipeline that extends autoregressive decoding for delay-pattern de-interleaving and multi-stream sampling, enabling efficient unified audio understanding and generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有高吞吐推理引擎缺乏对多模态生成的原生支持，尤其在语音语言模型中，多层音频令牌的生成与单流循环冲突。本文提出基于vLLM的推理流水线，通过扩展自回归解码实现延迟模式解交织和协调多流采样，并集成片上GPU声学解码器。实验表明该方法在不牺牲吞吐量的情况下支持统一的语音理解和生成，突破了CFG降低吞吐量的直觉。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有高吞吐推理引擎缺乏对多模态生成的原生支持，尤其在语音语言模型中，多层音频令牌的生成与单流循环冲突。本文提出基于vLLM的推理流水线，通过扩展自回归解码实现延迟模式解交织和协调多流采样，并集成片上GPU声学解码器。实验表明该方法在不牺牲吞吐量的情况下支持统一的语音理解和生成，突破了CFG降低吞吐量的直觉。
